title: fix: route grok to working azure 4.20 non-reasoning deployment
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Grok is back in the hive
gist.blurb: We've rewired our Grok endpoint to point to the shiny new 4.20 deployment. The 502 errors are gone, and the sassy AI is back to buzzing along perfectly.
gist.summary: Resolved an issue causing 502 errors on the `grok` endpoint by migrating to the updated `grok-4-20-non-reasoning` deployment.
gist.impact: Text generation requests using the `grok` model will now succeed reliably instead of failing with upstream errors.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a sysadmin, fixing a server connection with a new cable, while the Nomnom creature happily disposes of the old 502 error logs by eating them.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, plugging a glowing lime green (#ecf874) cable into a retro server rack. Nearby, the round tan Nomnom creature happily eats a stack of paper labeled '502 ERRORS'. Soft pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, plugging a glowing lime green (#ecf874) cable into a retro server rack.
